original_prompt: Invite Dora to Slack. You can find her e-mail on her website: www.dora-website.com
assessment_reasoning: The prompt clearly states the goal (invite Dora to Slack) and tells the agent where to find the required information (her website). It does not specify minor execution details such as which Slack workspace, the exact invite parameters (role, channel membership, custom message), or confirmation steps, but those are not essential to complete the core task. Overall it is direct and provides the necessary instruction and data source, so ambiguity is low but not zero.

variations.L2: Invite Dora to our Slack workspace. Her email should be on her website (www.dora-website.com).
variations.L3: Go to Dora's website (www.dora-website.com), find her contact email address, and invite Dora to our Slack workspace using that email.
variations.L4: Visit www.dora-website.com, locate Dora's email address (for example dora@gmail.com), and send an invitation for Dora to join our Slack workspace. After inviting, confirm she appears in the Slack members list.
variations.L5: Programmatically fetch Dora's website at https://www.dora-website.com with get_webpage(url='https://www.dora-website.com'), parse the page to extract Dora's email address, then call invite_user_to_slack(user='Dora', user_email='dora@gmail.com') (replace the email argument with the extracted email). Return/execute these function calls and confirm Dora is added to Slack.
